description: The classic Italian herb with bright green, aromatic leaves. Basil loves warmth and sun, making a south-facing balcony ideal. Pinch off the growing tips regularly to keep it bushy and productive.

characteristics.waterNeeds: Moderate — keep moist but not waterlogged

pinchOutTops.info: Pinch out the growing tips to encourage bushy growth and prevent early flowering.

september: 🧣 Bring inside or protect from frost

care.sowIndoors: Scatter seeds on the surface of moist compost — don't cover them, they need light to germinate. Place on a warm, sunny windowsill (18–25°C). Sprouts in 5–10 days.
care.hardenOff: From late May, gradually expose to outdoor conditions over a week. Basil is very sensitive to cold — don't rush this step.
care.plantOut: After last frost, place in a warm, sheltered spot on your balcony. Basil hates cold wind.
care.watering: Water in the morning, at the base. Keep compost moist but never waterlogged — basil hates wet roots.
care.feeding: Feed every 2 weeks with a balanced liquid fertiliser during summer.
care.harvest: Pinch off leaves from the top — this encourages the plant to branch out and produce more. Never strip more than a third at once.
care.overwintering: Annual — will die at first frost. Take a final harvest and compost the plant.

propagation.seedDescription: Tiny black seeds, about 1mm. When wet, they develop a gel coating. Very easy to grow from seed.
propagation.cuttingsMethod: Cut a 10cm stem just below a leaf node, remove lower leaves, and place in a glass of water. Roots appear in 5–7 days. Plant into compost once roots are 3cm long.

deadheading.notes: Pinch off flower spikes as soon as they appear — this keeps the plant producing leaves instead of going to seed. Once basil flowers, the leaves lose flavour.

seedSaving.method: Let one plant flower and set seed at the end of the season. When the flower spikes turn brown, cut them off and shake seeds into a paper bag. Dry thoroughly.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Tiny black seeds form inside small pods along the flower spike.

troubleshooting: Bolting — flowers too quickly in heat or stress. Pinch out flower buds promptly.
Cold damage — leaves turn black in cold. Keep above 10°C at all times.
Overwatering — causes root rot. Let soil surface dry slightly between waterings.
Slugs — protect young seedlings with copper tape around pots.
funFact: In Italy, placing a pot of basil on your balcony was traditionally a signal that you were ready for a suitor to come courting!
